name: Guadalest — Circular through the village and the reservoir
name_original: Guadalest — Circular por el pueblo y el embalse
region_intro: Hiking around El Castell De Guadalest offers diverse terrain characterized by dramatic rocky crags, the turquoise Guadalest Reservoir, and surrounding mountain ranges including the Sierra de Aitana. The region features valleys with pine trees, olive and almond groves, and limestone formations. This landscape provides varied elevation changes and natural features for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around El Castell De Guadalest The most popular hiking route is Guadalest — Circular through the village and the reservoir, a 3.3 miles (5.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 42 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the reservoir and the historic village. Another top favourite among local hikers is Guadalest Reservoir – Guadalest Castle loop from el Castell de Guadalest, an easy 3.6 miles (5.9 km) path. This trail combines reservoir views with an ascent towards the historic castle, providing panoramic vistas. Local hikers also love the Restaurante Rincón de Pepe – The cliffs of Pena Roc loop from Rincón de Pepe, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through the cliffs of Pena Roc, often completed in about 4 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around El Castell De Guadalest is defined by rocky crags, the Guadalest Reservoir, and mountain ranges. The network offers options for easy strolls, moderate excursions, and more challenging mountainous hikes. The routes in El Castell De Guadalest are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 1700 hikers have used komoot to explore El Castell De Guadalest's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around El Castell De Guadalest?
A: There are over 20 hiking routes in the El Castell De Guadalest area, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix of easy strolls, moderate excursions, and more challenging mountainous hikes.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly hiking routes in El Castell De Guadalest?
A: Yes, several routes are suitable for families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31468907" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir – Guadalest Castle loop from el Castell de Guadalest&lt;/a&gt;. This easy 5.9 km path combines beautiful reservoir views with an ascent towards the historic castle, offering panoramic vistas that children and adults alike will enjoy.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on a hike in this region?
A: The region is characterized by dramatic rocky crags, the stunning turquoise &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and surrounding mountain ranges like the Sierra de Aitana. You'll also encounter valleys with pine trees, olive and almond groves, and unique limestone formations. The reservoir itself is often called 'The Emerald Jewel' due to its vibrant color.

Q: Are there any circular walks around El Castell De Guadalest?
A: Absolutely. Many routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular choice is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870130884/guadalest-circular-through-the-village-and-the-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest — Circular through the village and the reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.4 km long and offers views of both the historic village and the reservoir.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions to see while hiking?
A: Beyond the breathtaking natural scenery, you can explore historical sites. The village itself, perched on a rocky crag, features the ancient Castillo de San José. Many trails offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and you might even spot the distant sea from higher viewpoints. For a unique natural attraction, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Springs Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, which are a short distance away.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, the region offers several difficult routes. For a more strenuous adventure, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40385581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The cliffs of Pena Roc – View of El Penyó loop from Rincón de Pepe&lt;/a&gt;. This 14.5 km trail involves significant elevation changes and takes you through rugged terrain, providing a rewarding challenge.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in El Castell De Guadalest?
A: The trails in El Castell De Guadalest are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning panoramic views of the reservoir and surrounding mountains, the unique historical atmosphere of the village, and the diverse terrain that keeps every walk interesting.

Q: Can I hike along a river in El Castell De Guadalest?
A: Yes, you can. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10356428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest River – Guadalest River loop from El Riu&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.6 km route that follows the Guadalest River, offering a different perspective of the area's natural beauty.

Q: Are there any longer, moderate hikes available?
A: For a longer, moderate hike, you could try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/18937416" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Restaurante Rincón de Pepe – The cliffs of Pena Roc loop from Rincón de Pepe&lt;/a&gt;. This 11.9 km trail leads through the cliffs of Pena Roc and typically takes around 4 hours 20 minutes to complete, offering a good balance of distance and manageable difficulty.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Castell De Guadalest?
A: El Castell De Guadalest offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring (March to May) is ideal with wildflowers and milder temperatures. Autumn (September to November) also provides comfortable weather and beautiful scenery. While summer can be hot, the mountain ranges offer a cooler escape, and the reservoir is perfect for water activities. Winter hiking is also possible, with crisp air and fewer crowds.

Q: Is the Guadalest Reservoir accessible for activities other than hiking?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a central feature of the valley and is popular for activities beyond hiking. During warmer months, its crystal-clear waters are suitable for kayaking and paddleboarding, offering a unique way to experience the 'Emerald Jewel' up close.
tips: Guadalest is a municipality in the Valencian Community, Spain, located in the interior of the province of Alicante, in the Marina Baja region. It has been declared a Historic-Artistic Site since 1974 and is part of the Association The most beautiful towns in Spain.

Stunning views from the viewpoints to the Reservoir.
The Guadalest reservoir is a reservoir close to the town of Guadalest, in the province of Alicante. It mainly receives water from the Guadalest River, a tributary of the Algar.
